title: Pi-hole Operations Dashboard
description: Infrastructure operations view for the Beelink-hosted Pi-hole service. Pi-hole application analytics are not exported, and container-specific resource metrics are prepared through OTel Docker Stats but not live-proven.

panels.title: Service Ownership and Recovery Reference
panels.description: Customer-facing DNS ownership and recovery context.

options.content: Owner: Family Platform. Production DNS runs on Beelink at 192.168.50.127. Raspberry Pi rollback DNS host remains 192.168.50.67. Use the Operations Dashboard runbook for validation and rollback gates.

panels.title: Beelink Host Up
panels.description: Host scrape state supporting the Pi-hole service.

panels.title: Pi-hole Container Metrics Status
panels.description: Current limitation caused by Docker 29/containerd cAdvisor compatibility.

options.content: Pi-hole container presence, CPU, memory, network, and block I/O are **unavailable pending a compatible Docker-container metrics exporter**. PLAT-13.6.3B prepares OTel Docker Stats through a restricted Docker API proxy, but live metric inventory must prove Compose identity before these panels become operational.

panels.title: Host Capacity Signals Affecting Pi-hole
panels.description: Host CPU, memory, and root filesystem signals that can affect DNS service reliability.

panels.title: Prometheus Scrape Health
panels.description: Prometheus target states. cAdvisor up does not prove Docker-container discovery.

panels.title: Future DNS and Admin Availability Checks
panels.description: Placeholder for future probe integration. This panel avoids fabricated healthy values.

options.content: DNS service availability and Pi-hole admin interface availability are not exported to Prometheus yet. Future work may add governed probes or an approved Pi-hole exporter. Until then, use the live runbook checks for DNS, blocking, and admin UI validation.

panels.title: OTel Docker Stats Target
panels.description: Prepared replacement source for Pi-hole container identity and resource panels. No data is expected before live deployment.

panels.title: Provisional Pi-hole Container Panels
panels.description: Panel acceptance requirements for OTel Docker Stats.

options.content: Planned panels: Pi-hole container presence by Compose service or container name, CPU, memory, network receive/transmit, and block I/O. Final PromQL must use live OTel metric inventory and must not fabricate Pi-hole application analytics such as DNS totals, block-rate summaries, client views, ranked domains, DNS probes, or admin-page probe status.
